neighborhoods.name: Midtown Manhattan
neighborhoods.description: Grand Central Terminal, Times Square, Bryant Park, and the densest commercial-residential mix in the country with all subway lines converging

housing.predominantType: High-rise condos, doorman rentals, and pre-war apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Manhattan. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Park Slope, Brooklyn
neighborhoods.description: Brownstone neighborhood adjacent to Prospect Park with family-oriented streets, food coops, and the F/G/Q/R subway lines

housing.predominantType: Brownstones, pre-war co-ops, and converted townhouses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Manhattan's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Astoria, Queens
neighborhoods.description: Greek, Brazilian, and Bangladeshi enclaves with significantly lower rent than Manhattan, the N/W trains to Midtown, and Astoria Park along the East River

housing.predominantType: Mid-rise rentals, walk-ups, and small co-ops

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Upper West Side, Manhattan
neighborhoods.description: Riverside Park, Central Park West, well-rated public schools, and classic doorman buildings on Broadway, West End, and Riverside

housing.predominantType: Pre-war doorman co-ops and condos, brownstones

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
